templates.name: Block Injection And Code Execution Risk
templates.description: Block builds or review gates when high-confidence injection or code-execution classes are present at high severity.
rationale: Injection and code-execution paths frequently translate directly into confidentiality, integrity, or availability compromise.
High-severity findings in these classes are usually strong candidates for stop-the-line review or release blocking.
recommended_workflow: Route findings to security review before merge or release.
Require validated remediation and rerun scanning after fixes land.

references.label: OWASP ASVS 5.0 Validation, Sanitization and Encoding Requirements
CWE Top 25 Most Dangerous Software Weaknesses

sources.label: OWASP ASVS 5.0 Validation, Sanitization and Encoding Requirements
CWE Top 25 Most Dangerous Software Weaknesses

templates.name: Warn On Identity And Access Control Drift
templates.description: Warn when authorization, authentication, and audit controls show medium-or-higher weakness patterns that should be fixed before release hardening completes.
rationale: Identity and access-control weaknesses often accumulate into exploitable chains even when individual findings are not release blockers by themselves.
Teams benefit from a visible warning policy that pushes these findings into remediation planning rather than letting them drift indefinitely.
recommended_workflow: Open tracked remediation work before release sign-off.
Require an owner and due date for unresolved warnings in this class.

templates.name: Block Exposed Secrets And Hardcoded Credentials
templates.description: Block release progression when committed secrets or hardcoded credentials are detected, regardless of whether they came from app code, config, or integration helpers.
rationale: Committed secrets are immediately reusable by anyone who can access the repository, artifacts, or leaked history.
The right operational response usually includes rotation or revocation, which makes these findings appropriate release blockers.
recommended_workflow: Revoke or rotate exposed credentials immediately.
Confirm the secret has been removed from code and replacement delivery is managed outside source control.
